title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. approximatum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. approximatum, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant properties. Hieracium vulgatum subsp. approximatum is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and its leaves and roots can also be used in herbal preparations such as infusions and salves.
